essence: Produces precision components by setting up, programming, and operating CNC machining equipment.

identity.title: CNC Machinist
identity.summary: Sets up CNC machines, selects tooling, interprets technical drawings, and produces high‑precision components for engineering and manufacturing industries.

salary.notes: Machinists with multi‑axis, aerospace, or complex programming expertise can exceed £55k-£70k+.

qualifications: Training in CNC machining, mechanical engineering, or precision manufacturing
Ability to set up CNC lathes, mills, or multi‑axis machines
Knowledge of tooling, feeds, speeds, and machining strategies
Understanding of tolerances, materials, and quality‑control processes

lifestyle.description: CNC machinists work in precision engineering firms, aerospace, automotive, and industrial manufacturing. They set up machines, produce components, inspect quality, and optimise machining processes. The role suits individuals who enjoy precision, problem‑solving, and working with advanced machinery.

habits: Sets up CNC machines and selects tooling
Produces precision components to tight tolerances
Performs quality checks and maintains equipment
